what_it_does: Implements a file-discovered registry for local Claude-Code ROUTINE definitions. It loads one self-registering Python file per routine from a routines/ directory, validates each module-level ROUTINE dict against a closed schema, and exposes the resulting Routine objects through a dict-like RoutineRegistry for listing/lookup by other components.
observations: A ROUTINE is a named, repeatable Claude-Code task fired by spawning a real claude -p session and injecting its prompt.
The registry mirrors the file-discovered registry pattern used in runtime/roles.py and runtime/registry.py.
Each routine file must declare a module-level ROUTINE dict; the file's id must equal the module name.
Validation is strict and fail-loud: missing/unknown fields, type mismatches, and duplicate ids all raise.
The module only registers and validates routines; actual firing, scheduling, and MCP tooling live elsewhere.
Fresh discovery is performed on every routine_registry() call, not via a cached singleton.
The ROUTINE schema includes optional cadence, repeats, goal, done_when, max_turns, and trigger fields.
standouts: Fail-loud closed-schema validation with explicit unknown-field rejection.
Id must equal the filename, making routines addressable by file.
Registry is deliberately decoupled from runner and trigger arms.
importlib.util is used to load routine modules dynamically from disk.
conventions: Closed schema with explicit ROUTINE_FIELDS frozenset.
Fail-loud validation rather than silent skipping.
__slots__ on the data class to keep instances lightweight.
Fresh factory function instead of a global singleton.
concerns: Dynamically executing arbitrary .py files in routines/ via importlib.util.exec_module could run untrusted code.
No validation of permission_mode values or cadence format beyond type checks.
No caching means repeated registry construction re-reads the filesystem.
No thread-safety mechanisms are visible in discover/rediscover.
notes: A strict, file-discovered registry for local Claude-Code task routines that validates a closed schema and exposes read/query access while leaving execution and scheduling to other modules.
questions: How is the routines/ directory path configured in different deployments?
What is the exact contract between this registry and runtime/routine_runner.py?
How is the cadence string translated into a systemd timer or periodic trigger?
Which component consumes Routine.record and what fields does it require?
Are routine modules allowed to have import side effects, and how is that sandboxed?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it is only the registry layer and implements discovery, validation, and lookup exactly as described.
intention_signals: To create a durable, local, file-driven catalog of repeatable Claude-Code tasks with strict schema discipline, while keeping the actual session spawning, scheduling, and manual firing concerns in separate modules.
novelty: Treats a spawned Claude-Code session as the execution unit and uses a local file-discovered registry as the canonical source of truth for such routines, intentionally mirroring but not duplicating cloud-resident scheduling.
contribution: Provides a validated, file-discovered routine registry with a closed schema and a dict-like API that runner, scheduler, and MCP tooling can consume to list and retrieve routines.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/routines.py is a file-discovered registry loader for local Claude-Code task routines, validating a closed ROUTINE schema and exposing query access to Routine objects.
intention_for_embedding: To give the Company a durable, local, self-registering catalog of repeatable Claude-Code tasks that can be listed, retrieved, and handed off to a separate runner or scheduler.

symbols.does: Data class representing one validated routine.
symbols.description: Stores a routine's id and raw spec dict, exposes typed property accessors for prompt, cwd, model, permission_mode, cadence, goal, done_when, repeats, max_turns, and produces an operator-facing record projection.

symbols.does: Initializes a Routine with id and spec.
symbols.description: Sets the routine id and stores the validated spec dictionary; uses __slots__ to keep the object minimal.

symbols.does: Returns the routine's required prompt string.
symbols.description: Accessor that yields the task text injected into a spawned Claude-Code session.

symbols.does: Returns the working directory for the routine.
symbols.description: Defaults to the Company repo root when no cwd is specified in the spec.

symbols.does: Returns an optional model override.
symbols.description: Accessor that yields the model string if set, otherwise None, allowing callers to override the default model for the spawned session.

symbols.does: Returns the permission mode for the routine.
symbols.description: Defaults to 'plan' (read/compute, no unattended edits) when not explicitly declared.

symbols.does: Returns the optional trigger cadence string.
symbols.description: Yields the systemd OnCalendar or 'every:<seconds>' string, or None for fire-only routines.

symbols.does: Returns the optional goal-loop description.
symbols.description: Provides operator-facing text describing what 'done' means for repeating goal-loop routines.

symbols.does: Returns the optional success predicate.
symbols.description: Yields a substring or regex pattern used by the goal-loop runner to decide when the routine has succeeded without a model judge.

symbols.does: Returns whether the routine is a goal-loop routine.
symbols.description: Boolean accessor defaulting to False; when True the runner/evaluator may re-fire the routine until done.

symbols.does: Returns the number of turns per fire.
symbols.description: Integer accessor defaulting to 1, controlling how many prompt/result turns the runner performs per invocation.

symbols.does: Produces an operator/tool-facing projection of the routine.
symbols.description: Returns a dict with id, label, description, prompt, cwd, model, permission_mode, cadence, repeats, max_turns, goal, done_when, and trigger for consumers like the MCP tool.

symbols.does: Dynamically loads a routine module from a file path.
symbols.description: Uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location and exec_module to load a .py file under a synthetic module name, returning its basename and the module object.

symbols.does: Validates a ROUTINE dict and builds a Routine instance.
symbols.description: Checks that the declaration is a dict, has a string id matching the filename, has a string prompt, contains only known ROUTINE_FIELDS, and satisfies type constraints for max_turns and cadence; raises on any violation.

symbols.does: File-discovered registry of Routine objects.
symbols.description: Maintains a mapping from routine id to Routine, supports discovery from one or more directories, rediscovery, and dict-like access via get, __getitem__, __contains__, and ids.

symbols.does: Initializes an empty registry.
symbols.description: Sets up empty internal storage for routines and the list of directories to scan.

symbols.does: Scans directories and populates the registry.
symbols.description: Loads each non-underscore .py file, extracts its ROUTINE dict if present, validates and builds a Routine, and fails loudly on duplicate ids.

symbols.does: Re-runs discovery using the previously set directories.
symbols.description: Convenience method to refresh the registry from the same dirs, picking up added or removed routine files.

symbols.does: Returns sorted list of registered routine ids.
symbols.description: Simple accessor used for listing and error messages.

symbols.does: Looks up a routine by id, returning None if missing.
symbols.description: Safe accessor that does not raise on unknown ids.

symbols.does: Looks up a routine by id, raising KeyError if missing.
symbols.description: Strict accessor that fails loud and lists registered ids in the error message.

symbols.does: Tests whether a routine id is registered.
symbols.description: Membership check against the internal routines mapping.

symbols.does: Factory that returns a freshly discovered RoutineRegistry.
symbols.description: Creates a new RoutineRegistry and calls discover with the provided directories or the default ROUTINES_DIR, ensuring routine drops are picked up without a process restart.
